question: B-6. Let \( n_{1}<n_{2}<n_{3}<\cdots<n_{k} \) be a set of positive integers. Prove that the polynomial \( 1+z^{n_{1}} \) \( +z^{n_{2}}+\cdots+z^{n_{k}} \) has no roots inside the circle \( |z|<(\sqrt{5}-1) / 2 \).

descriptive_long.question: B-6. Let \( firstidx<secondidx<thirdidx<\cdots<lastidx \) be a set of positive integers. Prove that the polynomial \( 1+complexvar^{firstidx}+complexvar^{secondidx}+\cdots+complexvar^{lastidx} \) has no roots inside the circle \( |complexvar|<(\sqrt{5}-1) / 2 \).

descriptive_long_confusing.question: B-6. Let \( peppermint<butterscotch<blackberry<\cdots<watermelon \) be a set of positive integers. Prove that the polynomial \( 1+magnolia^{peppermint}+magnolia^{butterscotch}+\cdots+magnolia^{watermelon} \) has no roots inside the circle \( |magnolia|<(\sqrt{5}-1)/2 \).
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
B-6 Let \( hummingbird(magnolia) \) denote the given polynomial. The power series expansion of \( 1/(1-magnolia)-2\,hummingbird(magnolia) \) has coefficients \( \pm 1 \) with leading coefficient -1. Hence,
\[
\left|1+ rac{1}{1-magnolia}-2\,hummingbird(magnolia)ight| \leqq |magnolia|+|magnolia|^{2}+\cdots =  rac{|magnolia|}{1-|magnolia|}.
\]
Also,
\[
 egin{aligned}
|2\,hummingbird(magnolia)| &\geqq \left|1+ rac{1}{1-magnolia}ight|-\left|1+ rac{1}{1-magnolia}-2\,hummingbird(magnolia)ight|\[4pt]
&\geqq 1+ rac{1}{1+|magnolia|}- rac{|magnolia|}{1-|magnolia|}=2\, rac{1-|magnolia|-|magnolia|^{2}}{1-|magnolia|^{2}}.
\end{aligned}
\]
The latter term is positive for \( |magnolia|<(\sqrt{5}-1)/2 \).

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Problem:
<<<
B-6. Let \( negativeone<negativetwo<negativethree<\cdots<negativevariable \) be a set of positive integers. Prove that the polynomial \( 1+realvariable^{negativeone} \) \( +realvariable^{negativetwo}+\cdots+realvariable^{negativevariable} \) has no roots inside the circle \( |realvariable|<(\sqrt{5}-1) / 2 \).
>>>
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
Solution:
<<<
B-6 Let \( nonpolynomial(realvariable) \) denote the given polynomial. The power series expansion of \( 1 /(1-realvariable)-2 nonpolynomial(realvariable) \) has coefficients \( \pm 1 \) with leading coefficient -1 . Hence,
\[
\left|1+\frac{1}{1-realvariable}-2 nonpolynomial(realvariable)\right| \leqq|realvariable|+|realvariable|^{2}+\cdots=\frac{|realvariable|}{1-|realvariable|}
\]

Also,
\[
\begin{aligned}
|2 nonpolynomial(realvariable)| & \geqq\left|1+\frac{1}{1-realvariable}\right|-\left|1+\frac{1}{1-realvariable}-2 nonpolynomial(realvariable)\right| \\
& \geqq 1+\frac{1}{1+|realvariable|}-\frac{|realvariable|}{1-|realvariable|}=2 \frac{1-|realvariable|-|realvariable|^{2}}{1-|realvariable|^{2}}
\end{aligned}
\]

The latter term is positive for \( |realvariable|<(\sqrt{5}-1) / 2 \).
>>>

garbled_string.question: B-6. Let \( jzopqkea<lmvfsyzd<qntxrbli<\cdots<vihpswce \) be a set of positive integers. Prove that the polynomial \( 1+fqhvbnms^{jzopqkea} \) \( +fqhvbnms^{lmvfsyzd}+\cdots+fqhvbnms^{vihpswce} \) has no roots inside the circle \( |fqhvbnms|<(\sqrt{5}-1) / 2 \).

kernel_variant.question:
Let $d_{1},d_{2},\dots ,d_{m}$ be any distinct positive integers and put
\[
F(w)=1+w^{d_{1}}+w^{d_{2}}+\cdots +w^{d_{m}}.
\]
Prove that $F(w)$ has no zeros in the open disc $|w|<\dfrac{\sqrt{5}-1}{2}$. (The order of the exponents is irrelevant---they need only be different.)
kernel_variant.solution:
Let F(z)=1+z^{d_1}+\cdots +z^{d_m}, and set

  H(z)=1+\frac1{1-z}-2F(z).

Since 1/(1-z)=\sum _{n=0}^\infty z^n, we get

  H(z)=1+\sum _{n=0}^\infty z^n-2\Bigl(1+\sum _{i=1}^m z^{d_i}\Bigr)
       =\sum _{n=1}^\infty  c_n z^n,

where c_n=+1 if n\notin {d_1,\ldots ,d_m} and c_n=-1 if n\in {d_1,\ldots ,d_m}.  Hence |H(z)|\leq \sum _{n=1}^\infty |z|^n=|z|/(1-|z|) for |z|<1.

On the other hand,

  2F(z)=1+\frac1{1-z}-H(z),
  so |2F(z)|\geq \Bigl|1+\frac1{1-z}\Bigr|-|H(z)|.

Write r=|z|<1.  A direct computation shows for z=re^{i\theta },

  Re\frac1{1-z}=\frac{1-r\cos\theta }{1-2r\cos\theta +r^2} \ge \frac1{1+r},

whence

  Re\Bigl(1+\frac1{1-z}\Bigr)\ge1+\frac1{1+r}>0
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \Bigl|1+\frac1{1-z}\Bigr| \ge Re\Bigl(1+\frac1{1-z}\Bigr) \ge 1+\frac1{1+r}.

Therefore

  |2F(z)| \ge \Bigl(1+\frac1{1+r}\Bigr)-\frac{r}{1-r} \
            =2\frac{1-r-r^2}{1-r^2}.

Since 1-r-r^2>0 precisely when r<(\sqrt{5}-1)/2, we conclude |2F(z)|>0 for |z|<(\sqrt{5}-1)/2, hence F(z)\neq 0 there.  This completes the proof that 1+z^{d_1}+\cdots +z^{d_m} has no zeros in |z|<(\sqrt{5}-1)/2.

core_steps: Form Q(z)=1/(1−z)−2P(z), whose power-series coefficients are ±1.
Use the geometric series to bound |Q(z)| ≤ |z|/(1−|z|).
Show the obtained lower bound 2(1−|z|−|z|²)/(1−|z|²) is positive when |z| < (√5−1)/2, so P has no zeros there.

slot1.description: Strict ordering of the exponents; they only need to be distinct so the polynomial’s coefficients stay 0 or 1.

slot2.description: Choice of the complex variable symbol; any symbol would work.
